title: Duplicate Resources Rendered in Architecture Diagram
description: The same resource appears multiple times in the architecture diagram due to dual rendering as both a subgraph container and as a nested child node. This creates visual confusion and violates the principle of single responsibility.

implementation.description: Analyze generated Mermaid diagram to detect duplicate node IDs or labels
logic_steps: 1. Parse generated Mermaid diagram for node definitions
2. Extract all node IDs: id1[label], subgraph id1[label], etc.
5. Also check for duplicate labels (different IDs, same label)
examples: VM_PublicIP appears as both: id_1[VM_PublicIP] and id_2[VM_PublicIP]
developerVMNetInt rendered as subgraph AND as child node inside parent VM
machine_dev_vm_sg is both: subgraph id AND rendered inside another subgraph with same label

root_causes: Parent-child rendering logic: resource rendered once as subgraph container, again as nested child in parent
Circular hierarchy handling: VM → NIC → VM creates two rendering paths for same resource
Missing deduplication: render_node() called for both parent context and child list without checking if already emitted
Hierarchy reconstruction: emitted_nodes set doesn't prevent rendering same resource in multiple contexts
remediation: In generate_hierarchical_diagram.py render_compute_hierarchy(): deduplicate nodes before rendering nested children
Track emitted Mermaid node IDs (not just resource names) to prevent re-rendering
Implement pre-rendering validation: scan parent-child relationships for circularity
Flatten circular hierarchies: keep resource in ONE context (either as container OR as child, not both)
Add test: verify each resource appears exactly once in generated diagram
